event_analyses.executive_summary: PROVE-USDT-SWAP triggered dual critical alerts on March 27: OI exceeded 24H average by 188.16% AND breached platform limit at 112.65% utilization. Current price $0.2703 (+1.96% 24H) with 5.44M contracts OI. Negative funding rate (-0.00122) suggests market is net short with funding flowing to longs. Top position holders are concentrated CN accounts using high leverage. The limit breach requires immediate parameter review.
event_analyses.forward_looking: If OI continues expanding above 100% platform limit, consider tightening position limits or temporarily restricting new opens. Monitor funding rate — persistent negative funding may attract more shorts, further pressuring the limit. Recommend reviewing tier structure given depth of only $48K.

event_analyses.executive_summary: NFLX-USDT-SWAP OI spiked 632.38% above 24H average on March 27, the highest OI deviation in today's alerts. Price relatively stable at $93.32 (+0.47%). OI stands at 312.26 contracts with near-zero funding. Key driver: VIP9 market maker (user 47180145) has held a large NFLX LONG since March 13. Multiple Vietnam-based retail shorts opposing the institutional long.
event_analyses.forward_looking: Monitor whether market maker reduces NFLX LONG position — unwinding could sharply reduce OI and trigger stop-losses on short-side retail positions. Vietnam retail short accounts (86067447, 61840902) are exposed if NFLX price rises. Near-zero funding indicates the long-short is roughly balanced in funding terms despite heavy OI concentration.

Check if legitimate long-side orders are being blocked. Monitor user 5001716 who deposited yesterday and holds XAU shorts.

**Summary:** 4 alert types analyzed. 0 flagged alert(s) parsed. 5 suspicious user(s) highlighted.
**Source document:** [PROD]Index Alarm - 2026-03-27 (00:00 - 23:59)

## Suspicious Users
- 615117494562816 — T3 — Multiple PROVE shorts on March 27 during 188% OI spike + 112.65% limit breach. High-churn CN account.
- 584491672416256 — T3 — Only LONG on PROVE in top 20, opened midnight March 27. Equity depleted to $74.
- 785142243199033355 — T3 — New Vietnam account (Dec 2025), $10K first deposit, NFLX short during 632% OI spike. Aggressive new account pattern.
- 211339444467404800 — T2 — XAU short positions during Z-cap event. Deposited yesterday. 7-year dormant account reactivated 2024.
- 516094364179638834 — T1 — VIP9 market maker (Cayman). NFLX LONG since March 13. Likely driving 632% OI spike via institutional hedging.

## Deep Profiles
- 615117494562816 — T3 — Long-standing CN account (2017) with extremely high trade/equity ratio ($157M vol vs $2K equity = 77,000x). Accumulated multiple PROVE short positions on March 27 during the critical OI spike event. Pattern suggests high-leverage perpetual trading with PROVE as a key instrument.
- 584491672416256 — T3 — CN account (2018) holding the only LONG position on PROVE among top 20 holders, opened at midnight March 27 (00:01 HKT). Contrarian position during critical OI spike. Very low equity ($74) with $1.78M trade history suggests account has depleted significantly. KYC2 only (not upgraded).
- 785142243199033355 — T3 — Very new Vietnam account (registered December 2025, 3.5 months old) with anomalously large first deposit ($10,000) relative to account age. Holding NFLX SHORT during the 632% OI spike. Vietnam accounts with large first deposits and rapid high-volume trading are a known risk pattern. KYC2 only.
- 211339444467404800 — T2 — Long-standing CN account (2017) with active XAU-USDT-SWAP short positions. Deposited yesterday (March 26) suggesting recent capital injection ahead of today's XAU Price Limit Z-cap event. Healthy trade vol ($48.5M) with moderate equity ($5.9K). Interesting gap: account registered 2017 but first deposit only in April 2024.
- 516094364179638834 — T1 — VIP9 market maker account (Cayman Islands, UK phone) with $8.46 trillion cumulative trade volume. Holding NFLX LONG since March 13 as part of hedged market-making activity. The 632% NFLX OI spike is likely attributable to this institutional participant's activity. Known entity, expected behavior — monitoring for position size changes.

rule_blocks.title: Buffer Too Tight
rule_blocks.description: Flags instruments where the EMA-derived limit price is already breaching or very close to the Y cap boundary, indicating the buffer is too tight for current market conditions.

rule_blocks.title: Asymmetric Basis
rule_blocks.description: Flags perp instruments where the EMA basis is large relative to the Z cap, suggesting the cap should be widened on one side to accommodate the persistent premium/discount.

rule_blocks.title: Asset-Type Consistency
rule_blocks.description: Checks whether each instrument's Y/Z caps are consistent with the standard ranges for its asset type classification.

rule_blocks.title: Z Cap <= Y Cap
rule_blocks.description: The Z (index) cap must always be strictly greater than the Y (limit) cap. If Z <= Y the price limit hierarchy is broken.
